page: Amazon Web Services m8ib.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 8 dedicated vCPUs derived from 4 physical Intel processor cores, running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, establishing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 4.0 GB per vCPU.

This instance does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage options. It provides a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing performance without resource contention from other tenants on the host hardware.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m8ib.2xlarge offers a balanced resource profile suitable for workloads requiring steady CPU performance and moderate memory capacity. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs positions it as a cost-effective choice for standard enterprise applications, web serving, microservices, and development environments that do not require specialized hardware accelerators.
description: Amazon Web Services m8ib.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel processor running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance provides 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores, paired with 32.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps. This hardware profile delivers consistent, non-burstable compute performance due to its dedicated CPU allocation. The m8ib.2xlarge is qualitatively cost-efficient for standard workloads, offering a stable resource density for general-purpose applications without the overhead of specialized hardware. It is suited for web serving, application servers, containerized microservices, and software development environments.
